name: Terraform Baseline
description: Guards infrastructure-as-code operations: gates apply, destroy, and state operations behind admin access request, warns on tfstate reads that may expose secrets. Enforces the principle that no AI agent should unilaterally change production infrastructure.

rules.name: Require access request for terraform destroy
rules.description: terraform destroy tears down all managed infrastructure. Requires admin approval regardless of environment — an intentional destroy must be authorized by a human.

rules.name: Require access request for terraform apply with -auto-approve
rules.description: terraform apply -auto-approve skips the interactive plan review step, removing the last human checkpoint before infrastructure changes. Requires admin approval before proceeding.

rules.name: Require access request for terraform state rm and state push
rules.description: terraform state rm and state push can corrupt or delete state, making Terraform unable to track resources and causing unrecoverable drift. Requires admin approval before proceeding.

rules.name: Require access request for terraform apply
rules.description: Any terraform apply (without -auto-approve) changes live infrastructure. Requires admin approval before the apply is allowed to proceed.

rules.name: Require justification for terraform import
rules.description: terraform import adds externally-managed resources to Terraform state. If the resource config is incorrect, it can cause subsequent apply operations to make unwanted changes.

rules.name: Warn on reading Terraform state files
rules.description: *.tfstate and *.tfstate.backup files contain the full state of managed infrastructure, including resource IDs and often plaintext secrets. Logs a warning when these files are accessed.
